text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously bullish but a technical warning is flashing - sky-high funding rates suggest the crowd is leaning too long, too hard. For chip and tech names specifically, that's a real risk because crowded longs unwind fast when they do.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is bleeding - it's a 3x leveraged ETF and decay is eating you alive; your stop at $200 is the line, but the damage is real and margin amplifies it. SPAL (-13.72%) is a new leveraged ETF already down hard with a razor-thin stop at $21.38 - one bad tick and you're out, which is fine, but watch it closely. SMH (-2.5%) is the clean-hands version of the same chip pain - not urgent but drifting wrong.

Watch: Whether SMH can hold $620 support - if it cracks, SOXL accelerates lower fast. Also watch if funding rates cool or stay elevated heading into the holiday-shortened week.

Bottom line: Your leveraged chip exposure is the live grenade right now - respect those stops and don't average down on margin.
plays: SOXL: Stop is $200 - honor it, no exceptions. Down 14.5% on a 3x leveraged name on margin is a compounding disaster waiting to happen if you freeze.
SPAL: Stop is $21.38 and it's already at $21.70 - almost there. Keep the order live; this one moves too fast to manage manually.
No new buys today - funding rates are flashing overcrowded longs and a holiday-shortened week thins liquidity. Hold, watch your stops.
